channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: How Declan Rice defied Arsenal's set-piece coach to score sensational free kicks
item.description: It seems like Arsenal's set-piece coach cannot take credit for Declan Rice’s two amazing free kicks against Real Madrid in the Champions League.
item.dc:creator: Steve Douglas, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: After 2 years in an Argentine prison, former Masters champ Angel Cabrera is back at Augusta National
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — It has been six long years since Angel Cabrera walked the verdant green fairways of the Masters. Two were spent in a gray Argentine prison cell. Once feted with a parade through the streets of Cordoba for his U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Fans impressed with new Bees Ballpark
item.description: SOUTH JORDAN, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Welcome to The Ballpark. The state of Utah has not had a brand new stadium since 2008 when Real Salt Lake opened Rio Tinto Stadium.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: With top seed in East secured, Atkinson wants to get some of Cavaliers' top players some rest
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Kenny Atkinson thought during training camp that realistic goals for the Cleveland Cavaliers would be 50 wins and a top-three seed in the Eastern Conference. The Cavaliers surpassed those goals awhile ago to the point where Atkinson is planning for a possible championship run.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Grizzlies' Jaylen Wells breaks his wrist after hard landing sends rookie to the hospital
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Memphis starting guard Jaylen Wells broke his right wrist on a hard fall on his arm and head from a midair collision at the end of a fast-break dunk during a victory over Charlotte on Tuesday, likely ending the rookie's season right before the Grizzlies go into the playoffs.
item.dc:creator: RICHARD WALKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cavaliers claim the No. 1 seed in the Eastern Conference with a 135-113 win over the Bulls
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Darius Garland scored 28 points, Evan Mobley had 21 points, 11 rebounds and seven assists and the Cleveland Cavaliers clinched the top seed in the Eastern Conference with a 135-113 win over the Chicago Bulls on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN DULIK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Rory McIlroy, still vying for first Masters title, wants to 'block out the noise' at Augusta
item.description: Rory McIlroy knows the conversation surrounding him as he heads into his 17th Masters Tournament, but the 35-year-old is "trying to block out the noise.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas' Arch Manning adds to growing NIL valuation, inks deal with clothing company
item.description: Arch Manning already holds one of the nation's highest collegiate athlete NIL valuations, and this week he increased that number by landing another brand deal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-MLB players Octavio Dotel, Tony Blanco among dead in Dominican Republic roof collapse
item.description: Octavio Dotel and Tony Blanco were among the former MLB players who were killed in the Dominican Republic roof collapse on Monday night, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nuggets fire coach Malone and won't extend GM Booth in stunning move as postseason looms
item.description: Michael Malone coached the Denver Nuggets to the NBA title in 2023. He has them set to make the postseason for a seventh consecutive year. Even amid a four-game slide, they're still in position to have home-court advantage in Round 1.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nuggets fire head coach, GM with three games remaining before the playoffs
item.description: The decision comes as the Nuggets only have three regular-season games remaining before the NBA playoffs.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, NewsNation

item.title: Walter Clayton Jr.'s defensive stop gives Florida its 3rd national title with 65-63 win over Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Florida’s Walter Clayton Jr. came up with the perfect going-away present for that spirit-crushing Houston defense that bullied, battered and bedeviled him all night. It was a defensive gem of his own. Right before the buzzer. For the win and the national title.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Steele tosses 7 innings as the Cubs pound the Rangers 7-0 on frigid night
item.description: Justin Steele tossed three-hit ball over seven innings, and the Chicago Cubs beat the Texas Rangers 7-0 on a frigid Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, WGN 9

item.title: Bears' Jaquan Brisker feels 'best I ever felt,' is eager to test Ben Johnson's offense
item.description: Jaquan Brisker is recovering from a concussion and is eager to face Ben Johnson's offense as the Bears begin their offseason workouts, with a focus on exercise and cognitive performance.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Sharkey, WGN 9

item.title: Torkelson's 2-run double in the ninth inning gives Tigers 4-3 win over White Sox
item.description: Spencer Torkelson smacked a two-run double to cap Detroit's three-run rally in the ninth inning and give the Tigers a 4-3 win over the Chicago White Sox on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Alex Ovechkin breaks Wayne Gretzky's NHL career goals record by scoring his 895th
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Alex Ovechkin fired just about the perfect version of his signature one-timer that has defined his remarkable career. When the puck hit the net, it made him the top goal scorer in NHL history.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Utah HC stays alive with 4-1 victory over Winnipeg
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Hockey Club is probably not going to make the NHL playoffs, but they are not going down without a fight. Karel Vejmelka made 32 saves for Utah in a 4-1 win against the the top team in the NHL, the Winnipeg Jets, at Delta Center on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Diego Luna's brace leads RSL past Galaxy, 2-0
item.description: SANDY, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Diego Luna scored two goals as Real Salt Lake cruised past the LA Galaxy Saturday afternoon at America First Field, 2-0. Luna scored the two goals in a five-minute span of the first half and Real Salt Lake (3-4-0, 9 points) controlled the rest of the contest.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Ian Happ plays in 1,000th major league game
item.description: Ian Happ played in his 1,000th major league game on Saturday — all with the Chicago Cubs. It's that last part that makes it special for the outfielder.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bulls Head Coach Billy Donovan joins Carmelo Anthony, Sue Bird and others in Basketball Hall of Fame class
item.description: Carmelo Anthony and Dwight Howard are going into the Basketball Hall of Fame later this year, not once but twice. And LeBron James and Chris Paul are part of the group that’s headed to the Hall as well, even before their playing careers end.
item.dc:creator: Tim Reynolds, WGN 9

item.title: Cleveland's José Ramírez hits 3 homers and claims another piece of Guardians history
item.description: José Ramírez hit three homers and carried the Guardians to a victory Friday night. The slugger claimed another place in Cleveland baseball history in the process.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Real Salt Lake hosts winless champs LA Galaxy in crucial western showdown
item.description: Real Salt Lake (2-4-0, T-11th place Western Conference) returns home Saturday afternoon when they host reigning MLS Cup Champions LA Galaxy at America First Field. RSL is coming off a road loss on Saturday night and hope to get their season back on track against a struggling LA side.
item.dc:creator: Mattey Casey, ABC 4

item.title: Vucevic, White, Giddey lead the post-season bound Bulls past the Trail Blazers, 118-113
item.description: Nikola Vucevic and Coby White each had 31 points, Josh Giddey had a triple-double and the post-season bound Chicago Bulls beat the Portland Trail Blazers 118-113 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Capitals beat Blackhawks 5-3, Ovechkin scores two goals to tie Gretzky
item.description: Alex Ovechkin tied Wayne Gretzky’s NHL record by scoring the 893rd and 894th of his career, the second being the game winner, as the Washington Capitals rallied to beat the Chicago Blackhawks 5-3 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Teams battle it out in NCAA Women's Final Four
item.description: The South Carolina Gamecocks are headed back to the NCAA Championship game to fight for their second straight national title.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Parker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Imanaga and Happ lead the Cubs past the Padres 3-1 in Wrigley Field opener
item.description: Shota Imanaga pitched one-run ball into the eighth inning in Chicago's home opener at Wrigley Field, and the Cubs handed San Diego its first loss with a 3-1 victory over the Padres on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Carpenter homers twice, Flaherty wins in Detroit return, Tigers beat White Sox 7-4 in home opener
item.description: Kerry Carpenter hit a pair of solo home runs and the Detroit Tigers beat the Chicago White Sox 7-4 Friday for their 14th win in their last 17 home openers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Kings score three times in third period to beat Utah
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Hockey Club's playoff chances are growing slimmer with each loss, and it looks like it's going to take a miraculous turn of events to get in now.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Red Rocks advance to NCAA regional finals
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The University of Utah gymnastics team is moving on to the NCAA regional finals after scoring a 197.625 on the first night of competition, tying with Denver University. Both the Utes and the Pioneers advance to Saturday's regional finals along with UCLA and Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Doug Bruno 'feels great,' but says stepping aside is the right call as Jill Pizzotti takes over at DePaul
item.description: CHICAGO – Less than a week after Hall of Fame head coach Doug Bruno stepped down for health reasons, the DePaul women’s basketball program named his successor—and it’s a familiar name to fans.
item.dc:creator: Josh Frydman, WGN 9

item.title: Two De Beque Dragons sign to college: Ava Vines and Allyson Sandidge
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — Despite having a population of less than 500, despite having just a small 1A school, De Beque, Colorado, and the De Beque Dragons have had a lineage of college athletes come through their program.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Report: Chris Collins agrees to multi-year extension with Northwestern
item.description: Northwestern University men's basketball coach Chris Collins has agreed to a multi-year contract extension, making him one of the top coaches in the Big Ten in terms of salary and compensation.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: ESPN commentator weighs in on NCAA Women's Final Four
item.description: Athletes from four collegiate teams are calling Tampa home for the next few days as the NCAA's Women’s Final Four returns.
item.dc:creator: Nyesha Harper, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How Mesa Juniors Volleyball Club brought home Gold in Las Vegas
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — Once high school volleyball season ends, several local high school athletes from all across the Western Slope come together to compete at the club level for the Mesa Juniors Volleyball Club.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Houston blows past the Jazz, 143-105
item.description: HOUSTON (ABC4 Sports) - The end of the season can't come soon enough for the Utah Jazz. Jalen Green scored 22 points, while Alperen Sengun was one assist away from a triple-double, as the Houston Rockets crushed the Jazz Wednesday night, 143-105.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
